growth_records.cite: Miyazaki J et al. Dual energy metabolism of the Campylobacterota endosymbiont in the chemosynthetic snail Alviniconcha marisindica. ISME J (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0605-7. [PMC7174374]
growth_records.method: not reported (metabolic-activity assay, not a growth-rate measurement)
growth_records.snippet: the average H2-consumption rate was calculated to be 15.7 +/- 8.23 umol/h/individual ... 0.59 +/- 0.45 umol/h/gill ... sulfide-consuming rates of kAlv individuals and dissected gill tissues were 20.3 +/- 6.4 umol/h/individual and 2.78 +/- 0
growth_records.notes: kAlv (Kairei-field) Alviniconcha marisindica holobiont carrying Sulfurovum sp. endosymbiont (16S closest to Sulfurovum sp. NBC37-1, 97.1%). Experimental substrate-consumption (uptake) rates from onboard incubations (Table 3), 5 C, filtered seawater. H2 supplied to ~100 uM headspace, sulfide ~200 uM. Rates reported per individual, per g individual, per gill, and per g gill tissue; the per-g-gill-tissue value is chosen as primary as the closest biomass proxy (0.11 umol/h/g gill for H2, 0.53 for H2S), other units listed in method. NOT normalizable to mmol/gDW/h (no bacterial dry-weight given). These are holobiont (host+symbiont) rates; culture_mode set to co-culture. No growth rate measured. Non-GEM/non-MFA field ecophysiology paper. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]

growth_records.cite: Miyazaki J et al. Dual energy metabolism of the Campylobacterota endosymbiont in the chemosynthetic snail Alviniconcha marisindica. ISME J (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0605-7. [PMC7174374]
growth_records.method: not reported (metabolic-activity assay, not a growth-rate measurement)
growth_records.snippet: the average H2- and sulfide-consuming rates were found to be 6.0 +/- 1.6 umol/h/individual for H2 and 9.8 +/- 0.35 umol/h/individual for sulfide and 0.11 +/- 0.21 umol/h/gill for H2 and 2.1 +/- 0.47 umol/h/gill for sulfide
growth_records.notes: eAlv (Edmond-field) A. marisindica holobiont carrying the same Sulfurovum endosymbiont species (16S 99.6% identical to kAlv). Experimental substrate-consumption (uptake) rates (Table 3), 5 C, filtered seawater. Per-g-gill-tissue chosen as primary (0.024 umol/h/g gill H2, 0.46 H2S); other units in method. eAlv shows ~1/3 the H2 consumption of kAlv, consistent with H2-depleted Edmond field. Holobiont rates; not normalizable to mmol/gDW/h. No growth rate. Non-GEM/non-MFA field ecophysiology paper. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]
